---
title: Sites
---

When you run `calepin site`, *Calepin* renders a collection of `.qmd` files into a structured website with navigation, sidebar, search, dark mode, and a consistent layout.


# Quick start

1. Create a `_calepin.yaml` in your project root:

```yaml
project:
  type: website

website:
  title: "My Site"
  pages:
    - index.qmd
    - about.qmd
    - section: "Guide"
      pages:
        - guide/intro.qmd
        - guide/advanced.qmd
```

2. Build the site:

```
calepin site
```

3. Preview locally:

```
calepin site --serve
```

The site is written to `_site/` by default.


# Configuration

The `_calepin.yaml` file (or `_site.yml`) configures your site. Here are the main sections:

## Project

```yaml
project:
  type: website
  resources:
    - images    # Directories copied to output
    - data
```

## Website metadata

```yaml
website:
  title: "My Site"
  subtitle: "A description"
  site-url: https://example.com
  favicon: assets/favicon.svg
```

## Navbar

```yaml
website:
  navbar:
    logo: assets/logo.svg
    logo-dark: assets/logo-dark.svg   # Dark mode variant
    logo-alt: "Site logo"
    background: "#fafafa"
    search: true                       # Enable search
    left:
      - text: "Home"
        href: index.qmd
    right:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/user/repo
```

Available icons: `github`, `twitter`, `x`, `bluesky`, `mastodon`, `linkedin`, `orcid`, `rss`, `email`, `globe`, `youtube`.

## Sidebar

```yaml
website:
  sidebar:
    collapse-level: 1    # 0 = all collapsed, higher = more open
```

## Pages

The `pages:` list defines the sidebar navigation tree:

```yaml
website:
  pages:
    - index.qmd                     # Simple page
    - text: "Custom Label"          # Page with custom nav label
      href: about.qmd
    - section: "Guide"              # Section with nested pages
      pages:
        - guide/intro.qmd
        - guide/advanced.qmd
        - section: "Subsection"     # Nested sections
          pages:
            - guide/sub/a.qmd
    - text: "External Link"
      href: https://example.com
```

Page titles are extracted from YAML front matter. The `text:` field overrides the title shown in navigation.


# Listings

A page can display a listing of other pages (for blogs, news, etc.):

```yaml
---
title: Blog
listing:
  contents: "posts/*.qmd"
  type: default           # default, table, or grid
  sort: "date desc"
---
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `contents` | Glob pattern for pages to list |
| `type` | Layout: `default` (cards), `table`, or `grid` |
| `sort` | Sort order: `"date desc"`, `"title asc"`, etc. |


# Output options

```yaml
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    highlight-style:
      light: github      # Light mode syntax theme
      dark: nord          # Dark mode syntax theme
    code-copy: true       # Show copy button on code blocks
    code-overflow: scroll
```


# Brand

Site-wide branding (colors, logos, typography) is configured under the `brand:` key:

```yaml
brand:
  color:
    primary: "#0066cc"
    background:
      light: "#ffffff"
      dark: "#1a1a2e"
    foreground:
      light: "#222222"
      dark: "#e0e0e0"
  logo:
    images:
      main:
        path: assets/logo.svg
        alt: "Logo"
    medium:
      light: main
      dark: main-dark
  typography:
    fonts:
      - family: Inter
        source: google
    base: Inter
    headings: Inter
```

Brand values are available in document bodies via the {% raw %}`{{ brand() }}`{% endraw %} function. See [Shortcuts](shortcuts.qmd#brand).


# Customizing site templates

Site templates control the HTML structure of every page. To override any template, create files in `_templates/` at your project root.

## Built-in templates

| Template | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `base.html` | Root layout: head, scripts, overall structure |
| `page.html` | Standard page (extends `base.html`) |
| `listing.html` | Listing page with card/table/grid (extends `base.html`) |
| `navbar.html` | Top navigation bar |
| `sidebar_left.html` | Left sidebar with page tree |
| `sidebar_right.html` | Right sidebar with table of contents |
| `search.html` | Search overlay modal |

## Template variables

Site templates receive two main context objects:

### site

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ site.title }}`{% endraw %} | Site title |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.subtitle }}`{% endraw %} | Site subtitle |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.url }}`{% endraw %} | Site URL |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.favicon }}`{% endraw %} | Favicon path |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.navbar }}`{% endraw %} | Navbar config (logo, left/right items, search) |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.sidebar }}`{% endraw %} | Sidebar config (collapse level) |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.pages }}`{% endraw %} | Navigation tree (with active page marked) |
| {% raw %}`{{ site.dark_mode }}`{% endraw %} | Whether dark mode is enabled |

### page

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ page.title }}`{% endraw %} | Page title |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.subtitle }}`{% endraw %} | Page subtitle |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.date }}`{% endraw %} | Page date |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.body }}`{% endraw %} | Rendered HTML content |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.toc }}`{% endraw %} | Table of contents HTML |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.url }}`{% endraw %} | Page URL path |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.source_url }}`{% endraw %} | Path to `.qmd` source file |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.listing }}`{% endraw %} | List of listing items (for listing pages) |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.breadcrumbs }}`{% endraw %} | Breadcrumb trail |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.prev }}`{% endraw %} | Previous page link (`text`, `href`) |
| {% raw %}`{{ page.next }}`{% endraw %} | Next page link (`text`, `href`) |

### Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ icon(name="github") }}`{% endraw %} | Insert an SVG icon by name |

## Example: custom page template

Create `_templates/page.html` to override the default page layout:

```html
{% extends "base.html" %}
{% block content %}
<article>
  <h1>{{ page.title }}</h1>
  {% if page.date %}<time>{{ page.date }}</time>{% endif %}
  {{ page.body }}
</article>
{% endblock %}

{% block page_nav %}
<nav class="page-nav">
  {% if page.prev %}<a href="{{ page.prev.href }}">Previous</a>{% endif %}
  {% if page.next %}<a href="{{ page.next.href }}">Next</a>{% endif %}
</nav>
{% endblock %}
```

The base template provides blocks you can override: `head_extra`, `content`, `page_nav`, `footer`.


# CLI

```
calepin site                    # Build to _site/
calepin site -o docs            # Build to docs/
calepin site --clean            # Clean output before building
calepin site --serve            # Build and serve locally
calepin site --serve --port 4000
calepin site -c my-config.yaml  # Use custom config file
```
